title: Discrete Mathematics_ Set Theory & Algebra - Partial Order

questions.question:
Let \( X = \{2, 3, 6, 12, 24\} \), Let \( \leq \) be the partial order defined by \( X \leq Y \) if \( x \) divides \( y \). Number of edges in the Hasse diagram of \( (X, \leq) \) is



GATE CSE 1996 | Question: 1.2

A partial order \( ≤ \) is defined on the set \( S=\left \{ x, a_1, a_2, \ldots, a_n, y \right \} \) as \( x \) \( \leq _{i} \) \( a_{i} \) for all \( i \) and \( a_{i}\leq y \) for all \( i \), where \( n ≥ 1 \). The number of total orders on the set S which contain the partial order \( ≤ \) is



GATE CSE 1997 | Question: 6.1

Let \( (S, \leq) \) be a partial order with two minimal elements a and b, and a maximum element c. Let P: S \(\to\) {True, False} be a predicate defined on S. Suppose that P(a) = True, P(b) = False and P(x) \(\implies\) P(y) for all \( x, y \in S \) satisfying \( x \leq y \), where \( \implies \) stands for logical implication. Which of the following statements CANNOT be true?



GATE CSE 2003 | Question: 31
options: P(x) = True for all x \(\in\) S such that x ≠ b
P(x) = False for all x \(\in\) S such that x ≠ a and x ≠ c
P(x) = False for all x \(\in\) S such that b ≤ x and x ≠ c
P(x) = False for all x \(\in\) S such that a ≤ x and b ≤ x
questions.correct_answer: P(x) = False for all x \(\in\) S such that a ≤ x and b ≤ x

questions.question:
The inclusion of which of the following sets into

\( S = \left\{ \left\{1, 2\right\}, \left\{1, 2, 3\right\}, \left\{1, 3, 5\right\}, \left\{1, 2, 4\right\}, \left\{1, 2, 3, 4, 5\right\} \right\} \)

is necessary and sufficient to make \( S \) a complete lattice under the partial order defined by set containment?



GATE CSE 2004 | Question: 73

Consider the set \( S =\{ a , b , c , d\}. \) Consider the following \( 4 \) partitions \( π_1,π_2,π_3,π_4 \) on
\( S : π_1 =\{\overline{abcd}\},\quad π_2 =\{\overline{ab}, \overline{cd}\}, \) \( \quad π_3 = \{\overline{abc}, \overline{d}\},\quad π_4 =\{\bar{a}, \bar{b}, \bar{c}, \bar{d}\} \).
Let \( \prec \) be the partial order on the set of partitions \( S' = \{π_1,π_2,π_3,π_4\} \) defined as follows: \( π_i \prec π_j \) if and only if \( π_i \) refines \( π_j \). The poset diagram for \( (S',\prec) \) is:



GATE CSE 2007 | Question: 26

Let \( \text{P} \) be the partial order defined on the set \( \{1,2,3,4\} \) as follows
\[
P=\{(x, x) \mid x \in\{1,2,3,4\}\} \cup\{(1,2),(3,2),(3,4)\}
\]

The number of total orders on \( \{1,2,3,4\} \) that contain \( \text{P} \) is __________.


GATE CSE 2024 | Set 2 | Question: 24

A partial order \( P \) is defined on the set of natural numbers as follows. Here \( \frac{x}{y} \) denotes integer division.

  1. \( (0, 0) \in P. \)
  2. \( (a, b) \in P \) if and only if \( (a \% 10) \leq (b \% 10 \)) and \( (\frac{a}{10},\frac{b}{10})\in  P. \)

Consider the following ordered pairs:

  1. \( (101, 22) \)
  2. \( (22, 101) \)
  3. \( (145, 265) \)
  4. \( (0, 153) \)

Which of these ordered pairs of natural numbers are contained in \( P \)?



GATE IT 2007 | Question: 23
